title: Fight Club
logline: An insomniac office worker forms an underground fight club with a charismatic soap maker, only to discover their organization has evolved into something far more dangerous than he ever imagined.
antagonistOrConflict: Tyler Durden / Jack's own fractured psyche
tone: Dark, satirical, nihilistic with moments of black comedy

actBreaks.description: Tyler holds a gun to convenience store clerk Raymond's head, marking the escalation from personal fight club to broader societal terrorism.

validation.ruling: Scene #63 shows Jack and Tyler's philosophical conversation at Lou's Tavern where Jack admits his condo explosion was the best thing that happened to him, marking his complete rejection of his materialistic lifestyle and entry into Tyler's world.

actBreaks.description: Jack realizes Tyler is his own split personality in the hotel room confrontation, fundamentally shifting the entire narrative reality.

validation.ruling: Scene #202 reveals the crucial twist that Tyler is Jack's split personality - Tyler explains that when Jack controls the body, he sees and hears Tyler but no one else does, fundamentally changing our understanding of the entire story.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script employs a sophisticated circular structure that begins and ends in the same high-rise social room, with Tyler holding a gun in Jack's mouth. The story functions as an extended flashback that gradually reveals its own unreliable narrator. The structure mirrors Jack's fractured psyche - starting with his mundane corporate existence, spiraling through the formation of fight club, then exploding into Project Mayhem's anarchistic terrorism. The revelation that Tyler is Jack's alter ego recontextualizes every previous scene, making this a story that demands re-examination of its own narrative foundation.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing accelerates dramatically after the apartment explosion (scene 31), with rapid-fire montages of fight clubs spreading and Project Mayhem activities. The middle section (scenes 84-172) maintains breakneck speed through escalating violence and chaos, while the final act slows for psychological revelation and confrontation. Short, punchy scenes averaging 1.1 pages create a staccato rhythm that mirrors Jack's fragmented mental state.

incitingIncident.description: Jack pairs with the dying Chloe who tells him she's improving, and he awkwardly tells her she looks like a pirate before confronting Marla outside.

validation.ruling: Scene #31 is just a brief imagination sequence where Jack encounters Marla at a cave entrance. Scene #33 is the true inciting incident where Jack confronts Marla outside the support group and they negotiate dividing up the groups, fundamentally disrupting his coping mechanism and forcing him into a new conflict that drives the story forward.

midpoint.description: Jack confronts his boss about the company's dangerous products and cover-ups, threatening exposure.

validation.ruling: Scene #155 shows Jack's transformation from reactive to proactive as he blackmails his boss with knowledge of corporate cover-ups, securing early retirement with pension. This represents his point of no return - he cannot go back to his old corporate life and has embraced Tyler's anti-establishment philosophy.

climax.description: Tyler seizes Jack's gun and shoves the barrel into Jack's mouth, threatening to kill them both as martyrs while Jack collapses exhausted to the floor in a room filled with duffel bags overlooking the city.

validation.ruling: Scene #251 is the ultimate confrontation where Jack shoots himself in the mouth to kill Tyler, his split personality. This resolves the main story question of Jack's identity crisis and represents the peak dramatic tension as he literally destroys his alter ego to save himself and stop the building explosions.

scenes.heading: INT. SOCIAL ROOM - TOP FLOOR OF HIGH-RISE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tyler holds a gun barrel in Jack's mouth as they struggle intensely, with Tyler speaking about immortality while Jack can only speak in vowels.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL GYMNASIUM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack attends a testicular cancer support group where men pair off and hug, and he embraces Bob who tells his life story.

scenes.heading: INT. JACK'S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack lies in bed unable to sleep, staring at the ceiling and swatting at a fly while hearing voices through the wall.

scenes.heading: INT. DOCTOR'S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Jack visits a doctor seeking medication for insomnia but is told he can't die from it and should get natural sleep instead.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY
scenes.summary: The doctor dismisses Jack's pain and sarcastically suggests he visit a testicular cancer support group to see real suffering.
scenes.turningPointNote: Doctor's sarcastic suggestion leads Jack to discover support groups

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL GYMNASIUM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack watches a dying speaker at the testicular cancer support group break down while talking about his ex-wife having a baby with her new husband.

scenes.heading: INT. GYM - LATER
scenes.summary: Jack pairs with Bob who embraces him and shares his pain about his grown children not returning his calls, making Jack feel deeply moved.

scenes.heading: INT. JACKS' BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack lies sound asleep in his bed, having become addicted to the support groups.

scenes.heading: INT. SMALL PROTESTANT CHURCH - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack joins a group hug at another support group, feeling more alive than ever.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE BUILDING BASEMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack embraces a weeping middle-aged woman at another support group and begins crying along with her.

scenes.heading: INT. CATHOLIC CATHEDRAL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack weeps while embracing a young man at a cathedral support group, feeling like the center of the world's attention.

scenes.heading: INT. PUBLIC BUILDING CONFERENCE ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A group leader guides everyone through a meditation to find their power animal in their secret cave.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENTRANCE OF CAVE (JACK'S IMAGINATION)
scenes.summary: In Jack's imagination, he approaches a cave entrance where a penguin emerges and tells him to slide.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack walks peacefully down the sidewalk after leaving a support group, feeling resurrected.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL GYMNASIUM - *RESUMING*
scenes.summary: Jack continues embracing Bob when Marla Singer enters the gymnasium asking if this is the cancer group while smoking a cigarette.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marla's arrival disrupts Jack's support group routine

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL GYMNASIUM - LATER
scenes.summary: Jack glares at Marla with hostility during the group session, recognizing her as a faker who attends multiple support groups without having any diseases.

scenes.heading: INT. SMALL PROTESTANT CHURCH - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack glares at Marla as she smokes during a melanoma support group meeting.

scenes.heading: INT. CATHOLIC CATHEDRAL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack opens his eyes during a meditation to glare at Marla who sits smoking with her eyes closed at a tuberculosis group.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL GYMNASIUM - RESUMING
scenes.summary: Jack and Marla make brief eye contact during the support group, and Jack realizes her presence makes him unable to cry or feel anything.

scenes.heading: INT. JACK'S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack lies awake on his bed holding a cordless phone, trying to resolve credit card balance transfers while surrounded by invoices.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Jack sits on the toilet flipping through IKEA catalogs, obsessing over furniture like the Njurunda coffee tables.

scenes.heading: INT. JACK'S LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack's empty living room is suddenly furnished with the Njurunda coffee tables he saw in the catalog.

scenes.heading: INT. JACK'S LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: More IKEA furniture appears in Jack's living room as he mentally catalogs each piece - sofas, armchair, lamps, clock, and shelving unit.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - RESUMING
scenes.summary: Jack flips to a kitchen spread in the IKEA catalog and notices his shift from reading Playboy to obsessing over furniture catalogs.

scenes.heading: INT. JACK'S KITCHEN AND DINING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jack walks through his fully furnished kitchen and dining room while still on the phone, examining his expensive dishware and realizing his possessions own him.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Jack lies in bed still on hold with customer service, navigating phone menus while planning to confront Marla at the next support group.

scenes.heading: INT. COPY ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Jack operates a copy machine at work with a Starbucks cup on top, feeling like everything is a copy of a copy due to his insomnia.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE AREA - DAY
scenes.summary: The office area is shown with its sterile corporate design of glass walls and industrial carpet.

scenes.heading: INT. JACK'S OFFICE - SAME
scenes.summary: Jack sits in his office staring blankly when a pudgy coworker enters with reports and tells him he needs to travel more for work.

scenes.heading: INT. SMALL PROTESTANT CHURCH - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack attends another support group where the dying Chloe speaks about her uncertain prognosis while Jack scowls at Marla's presence.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENTRANCE OF CAVE (JACK'S IMAGINATION)
scenes.summary: In Jack's power animal meditation, Marla appears at the cave entrance smoking and blowing smoke in his face instead of his penguin.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRUCH - RESUMING
scenes.summary: Jack's eyes snap open during meditation and he glowers at Marla who sits smoking with her eyes closed.

scenes.heading: INT. CHURCH - LATER
scenes.summary: Jack pairs with the dying Chloe who tells him she's improving, and he awkwardly tells her she looks like a pirate before confronting Marla outside.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jack directly confronts Marla about attending support groups

scenes.heading: EXT. CHURCH - NIGHT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jack and Marla negotiate which support groups each will attend, with Jack arguing he has more right to testicular cancer group.

scenes.heading: INT. LAUNDROMAT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marla takes clothes from unwatched dryers in a laundromat while continuing to negotiate support group territories with Jack.

scenes.heading: EXT. SIDEWALK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marla leads Jack into traffic where cars screech to avoid hitting them, then heads into a thrift store.

scenes.heading: INT. THRIFT STORE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marla sells the stolen clothes to a thrift store clerk while continuing to argue with Jack about support group assignments.

scenes.heading: EXT. SIDEWALK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jack catches up to Marla on the sidewalk and they exchange phone numbers, with Marla writing hers on his palm before walking into traffic again.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE CABIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Jack wakes up as his plane lands at O'Hare airport.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE CABIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Jack wakes up disoriented as his plane lands at SeaTac airport.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY - DUSK
scenes.summary: Jack stands by a wrecked car on the highway at sunset, marking notes on a document.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPORT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack arrives at an airport gate two hours early and adjusts his watch while contemplating how his life is ending one minute at a time.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE CABIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Jack wakes up as his plane lands at Air Harbor International.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPORT WALKWAY
scenes.summary: Jack rides an airport conveyor belt and briefly sees Tyler on the opposite belt moving past him.
scenes.turningPointNote: First appearance of Tyler in Jack's normal life

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE CABIN - IN FLIGHT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack sits on a plane next to a businessman, examining the single-serving items provided by the flight attendant.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL ROOM - BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack brushes his teeth in a hotel bathroom with single-use toiletries, then sits on the bed watching TV and finding a mint.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE CABIN - IN FLIGHT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack sits next to different passengers who keep changing as he takes bites of his meal, illustrating his single-serving friendships.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE CABIN - LANDING
scenes.summary: Jack wakes up as his plane lands at Logan airport.

scenes.heading: EXT. CONCRETE LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: Two technicians lead Jack to a warehouse containing a burnt-out wrecked automobile for inspection.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jack examines the car wreck while technicians point out where victims died, as he explains his job applying formulas to determine recall costs.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE CABIN - TAKING OFF - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack sits on a plane next to a lady who asks about his job, then experiences a detailed fantasy of the plane crashing and burning while he remains unharmed and emotionless, only to snap back to normal reality when the seatbelt light goes off.

scenes.heading: INT. BAGGAGE CLAIM AREA - WILMINGTON - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack waits in an empty baggage claim area while a security officer explains that his vibrating suitcase triggered a bomb scare, joking about electric razors and dildos being common causes.

scenes.heading: EXT. EMPTY RUNWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A solitary suitcase explodes on an empty runway.

scenes.heading: INT. BAGGAGE CLAIM AREA - RESUMING
scenes.summary: The security officer continues entertaining Jack with industry slang while on hold, then hangs up and shrugs.

scenes.heading: EXT. AIRPORT DRIVE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Jack waits for a taxi while contemplating how things could be worse, imagining spiders laying eggs under facial skin.

scenes.heading: INT. TAXI - MOVING - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack rides in a taxi toward his condo building and sees smoke and a blown-out section on the fifteenth floor where he lives.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jack's apartment has been destroyed, forcing him into homelessness and dependency on Tyler

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET IN FRONT OF BUILDING
scenes.summary: Jack stares at his destroyed building, pays the cabbie, and approaches the doorman who tells him he can't enter due to a police arson investigation.

scenes.heading: EXT. CONDO BUILDING - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jack walks through charred debris from his belongings while the doorman follows him to a payphone, and Jack's voiceover explains how someone could have turned off the pilot light.

scenes.heading: EXT. PAYPHONE - RESUMING
scenes.summary: Jack stares at the payphone numbers while the doorman philosophizes about young people buying too much, and Jack's voiceover continues explaining how gas could have filled his condo.

scenes.heading: EXT. PAYPHONE - RESUMING
scenes.summary: Jack's fingers hover over the phone numbers as the doorman continues talking, and Jack's voiceover describes the refrigerator compressor clicking and causing the explosion.

scenes.heading: EXT. PAYPHONE - RESUMING
scenes.summary: Jack finds Tyler's business card, dials the number, and waits as it rings while praying to be delivered from his material possessions, then hangs up when no one answers.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOU'S TAVERN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of Lou's Tavern, a small building in a concrete parking lot near a freeway.

scenes.heading: INT. LOU'S TAVERN - SAME
scenes.summary: Jack and Tyler sit in the back of Lou's Tavern drinking beer while Tyler explains how material possessions define and trap people, leading Jack to realize losing everything was the best thing that happened to him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Tyler begins his philosophical influence over Jack, reframing the apartment destruction as liberation

scenes.heading: INT. PROJECTIONIST ROOM - THEATRE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tyler works as a projectionist, explaining to Jack how he switches between two projectors at the exact moment to prevent audiences from seeing breaks between reels.

scenes.heading: INT. LARGE BANQUET HALL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tyler works as a banquet waiter at the luxurious Pressman Hotel, serving wealthy guests who treat the waiters contemptuously.

scenes.heading: INT. SERVICE ELEVATOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tyler positions himself to urinate into the soup while Jack looks away, but Tyler actually pours out a water glass while pretending to relieve himself.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKING LOT OF TAVERN - RESUMING
scenes.summary: Tyler asks Jack to hit him as hard as he can in the tavern parking lot, explaining they're both virgins to being hit, and Jack reluctantly agrees to try.
scenes.turningPointNote: The first fight between Jack and Tyler, establishing the foundation of Fight Club

scenes.heading: EXT. CURBSIDE - LATER
scenes.summary: Jack and Tyler sit on a curb in endorphin-induced serenity after their fight, discussing what they were fighting against and agreeing to do it again.

scenes.heading: EXT. PAPER STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of Paper Street showing an abandoned paper mill and one dilapidated three-story house.

scenes.heading: INT. PAPER ST. HOUSE - LIVING ROOM -SAME
scenes.summary: Tyler leads Jack up the staircase to a second floor room in the Paper Street house.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jack sits on an old creaky bed in the dusty room while his voiceover explains Tyler had been living there for half a year waiting for re-zoning.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOU'S TAVERN PARKING LOT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Long shot of six guys watching two men fight in Lou's Tavern parking lot.

scenes.heading: INT. PAPER ST. HOUSE - KITCHEN - MORNING
scenes.summary: Jack makes coffee in the broken-down kitchen, showing new bruises and cuts but smiling happily while shooing away cockroaches.

scenes.heading: EXT. CONVENIENCE STORE PARKING LOT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Long shot showing ten guys now watching a fight in a convenience store parking lot.

scenes.heading: INT. SHOWER - MORNING
scenes.summary: Jack tries to shower but the water system malfunctions, producing loud vibrations and only dribbling water.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - MORNING
scenes.summary: Tyler checks his briefcase of soap bars while Jack drinks black coffee and casually pulls a tooth from his mouth before they leave for work.

scenes.heading: EXT. CINEMA PARKING LOT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Long shot showing fourteen guys now watching a fight in a cinema parking lot.

scenes.heading: EXT. PORCH - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tyler and Jack sit on the porch drinking beer with fresh bruises when rain begins and Tyler gets up.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT - SAME
scenes.summary: Tyler and Jack stand knee-deep in basement flood water and coordinate flipping electrical breaker switches in a specific order.

scenes.heading: EXT. CONSTRUCTION AREA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Long shot showing eighteen guys now watching a fight in a construction area.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tyler and Jack sit with candles in the rain-leaking living room surrounded by thousands of magazines, reading articles written in first person about body parts.

scenes.heading: INT. CONFERENCE ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Jack sits in a dark conference room during a slide presentation about office efficiency, sporting a puffy lip and bruise while his boss looks concerned.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOU'S TAVERN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Multiple cars arrive simultaneously at Lou's Tavern and young men get out and enter, including guys who watched Tyler and Jack's first fight.

scenes.heading: INT. LOU'S TAVERN - SAME
scenes.summary: The bartender announces closing time to clear out regular customers while the arriving men wait eagerly, then Tyler and Jack enter and the group moves toward the basement.
scenes.turningPointNote: The formal establishment of Fight Club as an organized group activity

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT STEPS - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The men march down to the basement chatting excitedly about fighting and bringing new people to experience it.

scenes.heading: INT. TAVERN BASEMENT - SAME
scenes.summary: In the concrete basement, the men pair up eagerly under a single light bulb while Tyler moves to the center and his presence silences all chatter as he begins establishing the rules of Fight Club.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE PARK RESTAURANT - DAY
scenes.summary: Jack is served a refill by a broken-nosed waiter from Fight Club, reflecting on how Fight Club identities remain separate from regular life and how alive it makes them feel.

scenes.heading: INT. JACK'S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Jack plays solitaire at work while dabbing blood from his mouth as his boss questions what he's getting into, and Jack reflects on how Fight Club makes everything else feel turned down.

scenes.heading: INT. BUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Jack aggressively knocks back an arrogant man who bumped him on a bus, then exchanges knowing looks with another Fight Club member.

scenes.heading: INT. PARKING GARAGE - DAY (FLASHBACK)
scenes.summary: Jack sees a bruised valet who shares a smile with him, then watches the valet size up a fat executive and peel out aggressively in his BMW.

scenes.heading: INT. TAVERN BASEMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack fights an opponent in the basement, landing jabs and uppercuts while the crowd cheers maniacally in what Jack describes as tongues-speaking hysteria.

scenes.heading: EXT. BAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Everyone files out of the bar sweating, bleeding, and smiling, feeling saved according to Jack's voiceover.

scenes.heading: EXT. SIDEWALK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack and Tyler walk through the streets carrying wooden sticks, smashing car bumpers to set off alarms and airbags, vandalizing advertisements and road signs.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANOTHER STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jack and Tyler slow down from running, both panting and bloody, as Tyler declares that self-improvement is masturbation and self-destruction is the answer.

scenes.heading: INT. PAPER ST. HOUSE - KITCHEN - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Jack answers the phone in the Paper Street house kitchen while buttoning his shirt.

scenes.heading: INT. MARLA'S ROOM - SAME
scenes.summary: Marla calls Jack asking where he's been, wanting to join his new support group, but he tells her it's for men only and tries to end the conversation as she mentions going to debtors anonymous.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: Intense sexual activity occurs in dim light with glimpses of sweating bodies, torn sheets, bodies hitting the floor, and Marla's face visible during climax.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marla and Tyler begin their sexual relationship, complicating the Jack-Tyler dynamic

scenes.heading: INT. JACK'S BEDROOM - SUNRISE
scenes.summary: Jack sits up in his bed at sunrise and looks around his room.

scenes.heading: INT. 2ND FLOOR LANDING
scenes.summary: Jack steps out of his room and notices Tyler's door is closed for the first time in the month he's lived there.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - SAME
scenes.summary: Jack stares into the toilet and sees six used condoms floating in it.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - MORNING
scenes.summary: Jack sits at the kitchen table reading when Marla walks in disheveled, implying she spent the night with Tyler.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - LATE AFTERNOON (FLASHBACK)
scenes.summary: Tyler enters the kitchen and picks up the phone to listen to Marla's suicidal call.

scenes.heading: INT. 8TH FLOOR LANDING - LATE AFTERNOON (FLASHBACK)
scenes.summary: Tyler reaches Marla's floor and her hand shoots out to grab his arm.

scenes.heading: INT. MARLA'S ROOM - CONTINUOUS (FLASHBACK)
scenes.summary: Marla pulls Tyler into her room in a drugged state as cops and paramedics arrive outside for someone else.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY (FLASHBACK)
scenes.summary: Marla and Tyler sneak past the emergency responders and escape down the stairs.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - NIGHT (FLASHBACK)
scenes.summary: Tyler makes coffee while Marla tells him he must keep her awake all night to prevent her from dying.

scenes.heading: INT. TYLER'S ROOM - DAWN (FLASHBACK)
scenes.summary: Marla and Tyler lie in bed at dawn after having sex, with Marla claiming he's now responsible for her forever.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - MORNING - RESUMING
scenes.summary: Tyler describes his sexual relationship with Marla to Jack, calling it sport-fucking while Jack grows increasingly agitated.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack sits in the living room watching television at high volume while Tyler and Marla have loud sex upstairs.

scenes.heading: INT. JACK'S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack lies on his bed listening to the sounds of Tyler and Marla's violent sexual encounter through the wall.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack brushes his teeth and looks through the keyhole to see Tyler and Marla having sex.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack turns off the house's electrical fuses in the basement during a rainstorm.

scenes.heading: INT. 2ND FLOOR LANDING - SAME
scenes.summary: Jack walks upstairs and stops at Tyler's partially open door where Tyler catches him watching and offers to let him finish with Marla.

scenes.heading: INT. JACK'S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Jack sits at work in blood-stained clothes typing haikus when his boss tells him to go home and clean up.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - SAME
scenes.summary: Jack walks past hostile coworkers in the hallway, appearing passive about his bruised and bloodied appearance.

scenes.heading: EXT. PAPER STREET - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Jack walks toward the Paper Street house at sunset.

scenes.heading: INT. TYLER'S KITCHEN - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Jack enters the kitchen as Tyler and Marla have violent sex upstairs, causing plaster to fall from the ceiling.

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE STATION - OFFICE
scenes.summary: Detective Stern calls Jack to reveal his condo was destroyed by homemade dynamite and someone broke in using professional techniques.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jack learns his condo explosion was deliberate arson, escalating the mystery and his paranoia.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tyler leads Jack at a brisk pace across a street and into a wooded area.

scenes.heading: EXT. ALL-NIGHT CONVENIENCE STORE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Tyler pulls a handgun and holds it to a convenience store clerk's head in the parking lot.

scenes.heading: EXT. WOODED AREA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tyler forces the clerk at gunpoint to kneel in the woods, reveals he blew up Jack's condo, and threatens to kill the clerk unless he pursues his abandoned veterinary dreams.
scenes.turningPointNote: Tyler confesses to destroying Jack's condo and holds an innocent man at gunpoint, revealing his extreme methods and philosophy.

scenes.heading: EXT. FENCED-IN BIOHAZARD WASTE DUMP SITE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tyler and Jack climb over a fence into a biohazard waste dump site and hide from a security guard.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tyler stirs a boiling pot in the kitchen, explaining how fat renders and tallow floats to the surface.

scenes.heading: EXT. WASTE DUMP SITE - NIGHT (RESUMING)
scenes.summary: Tyler and Jack climb back over the fence carrying bags of stolen fat, with Jack's bag ripping and covering him in goo as they both start laughing.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - NIGHT (RESUMING)
scenes.summary: Tyler shows Jack two pots with separated tallow and explains the glycerin layer for soap making.

scenes.heading: EXT. WASTE DUMP SITE - NIGHT (RESUMING)
scenes.summary: Tyler and Jack fall to the ground laughing, slip in fat, and stagger away as the security guard approaches.

scenes.heading: INT. TAXICAB - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Jack and Tyler sit in a taxicab covered in fat and giggling while the cab driver stares at them.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tyler kisses Jack's hand and pours lye on it, burning his skin while delivering a philosophical speech about God and pain.
scenes.turningPointNote: Tyler deliberately burns Jack with lye in a ritualistic act, marking a shift toward more extreme psychological manipulation.

scenes.heading: INT. CAVE
scenes.summary: Marla appears in a beautiful black dress in Jack's hallucination or vision.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tyler continues his philosophical speech about God's hatred and indifference while Jack experiences pain and visions.

scenes.heading: INT. CAVE
scenes.summary: In Jack's vision, Marla pulls him down and he moves to kiss her.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tyler delivers his speech about being God's middle children while Jack struggles with pain and tears, ending with Tyler's declaration about death.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - SUNRISE
scenes.summary: Jack cuts perfectly shaped bars of soap while Tyler wraps them with Paper Street Soap Company labels.

scenes.heading: INT. BARNEY'S - DAY
scenes.summary: Jack and Tyler sell their soap to an upscale buyer at Barney's for twenty dollars per bar.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tyler and Jack walk down the street as Tyler delivers his speech about men being slaves to advertising and consumerism.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack confronts Bob outside a support group meeting, challenging his masculinity and starting a fight by punching him.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT - ELECTRONICS WAREHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Bob and Jack fight in a basement while a crowd of new members screams and cheers.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASEMENT DOOR - ELECTRONICS WAREHOUSE - LATER
scenes.summary: Bob and Jack emerge from the fight bloodied and bruised, with Bob thanking Jack before Jack collapses from exhaustion.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Ricky fights a yuppie on the street who tries to impale him on a fence spike before Tyler and Jack intervene.

scenes.heading: INT. TRUCK GARAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ricky and the yuppie continue fighting in a truck garage while someone places satirical bumper stickers on luxury cars.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack, Tyler and Ricky paste up a fake environmental billboard with dangerous misinformation about using motor oil as fertilizer.

scenes.heading: EXT. LARGE PARKING LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: Jack and Bob switch entrance and exit signs in a parking lot, causing a car's tires to explode on hidden blades.

scenes.heading: EXT. SIDEWALK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack encounters Marla leaving a support group, then joins Tyler and Bob as they head toward a park.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARK - OUTDOOR BEER FESTIVAL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack, Tyler and Bob topple a row of portable toilets like dominoes at a beer festival.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE CABIN - ON GROUND - DAY
scenes.summary: Two bruised airplane maintenance men insert fake safety cards showing passengers screaming in terror into seat back pouches.

scenes.heading: INT. PAPER ST. HOUSE - KITCHEN - MORNING
scenes.summary: Tyler gives Jack a sheet of paper to copy for expanding fight club operations.

scenes.heading: INT. PHOTOCOPY ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Jack operates a copy machine surrounded by other bruised coworkers, growing drowsy from the flashing lights.

scenes.heading: INT. JACK'S OFFICE - LATER
scenes.summary: Jack's boss confronts him about leaving fight club rules in the copy machine, leading Jack to threaten and blackmail his boss.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jack openly threatens his boss and uses fight club to gain power over his corporate life, showing his complete transformation.

scenes.heading: EXT. MORTUARY - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Jack enters a mortuary in the late afternoon.

scenes.heading: INT. MORTUARY - SAME
scenes.summary: Jack finds Marla working at the mortuary desk, telling a customer his urn is too small for his ashes.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARLA'S HOTEL - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Jack watches Marla take two boxes from a Meals on Wheels van.

scenes.heading: INT. MARLA'S ROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Marla reveals she steals meals meant for dead people because she's poor and hungry, eating while Jack watches.

scenes.heading: INT. MARLA'S ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack examines Marla's breast for lumps while she guides his hand, creating an intimate moment before she buttons her shirt.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Tyler appears and bumps into Jack on the street, leading him away while asking what he's doing.

scenes.heading: INT. BOSS'S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Jack confronts his boss about the company's dangerous products and cover-ups, threatening exposure.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jack openly threatens his corporate employer with exposure of deadly product defects, marking his complete break from his former life

scenes.heading: INT. PAPER ST. HOUSE - JACK'S ROOM - DUSK
scenes.summary: Jack wakes up bloody in bed and hears the back door slam.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Jack shows Tyler his paycheck while Tyler, dressed in combat gear and covered in paint, celebrates with beers.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM
scenes.summary: Jack discovers fight club members watching TV coverage of a building with a giant painted face and broken, smoking windows.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL BALLROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack works as a waiter at a luxury banquet, watching Commissioner Jacobs while Tyler and other fight club members pose as waiters.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - SAME
scenes.summary: Jack, Tyler, Bob and Ricky attack Commissioner Jacobs in the bathroom, taping his mouth shut.

scenes.heading: INT. SERVICE ELEVATOR - SAME
scenes.summary: The group drags Commissioner Jacobs into an elevator, pulls down his pants, and Tyler threatens castration unless he stops his investigation.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOTEL - LATER
scenes.summary: The group exits the hotel through the back service entrance and splits up in different directions.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack viciously beats Angel Face unconscious during fight club while Tyler watches with a stone face.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - LATER
scenes.summary: Jack tells Tyler he felt like destroying something beautiful as they approach a stolen car from a valet.

scenes.heading: INT. STOLEN CAR - MOVING - LATER
scenes.summary: Tyler drives recklessly in the rain while Jack confronts him about being excluded from Project Mayhem, then Tyler reveals they're in a car with defective parts.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY CAR (JACK'S IMAGINATION)
scenes.summary: Jack imagines the family that died in the flaming, tumbling car he saw in the warehouse.

scenes.heading: INT. TYLER'S STOLEN CAR - RESUMING
scenes.summary: The car crashes down a deep ravine and lands on its roof at the bottom.

scenes.heading: EXT. OVERTURNED CAR
scenes.summary: Tyler crawls out and drags Jack from the overturned car, telling him he just had a near-life experience.

scenes.heading: INT. JACK'S BEDROOM - LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tyler sits beside Jack's bed describing his vision of a post-apocalyptic world where people hunt elk through ruined cities.

scenes.heading: INT. JACK'S BEDROOM - DAWN
scenes.summary: Jack lies in bed staring at the ceiling while Tyler is gone and faint sounds of construction can be heard.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Jack sits broken in the kitchen while Marla enters with bruises, and they have an awkward conversation before Jack discovers the basement door is ajar.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jack discovers the basement filled with triple-decker bunks as the doorbell rings upstairs.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Ricky arrives dressed in black with a mattress, but Tyler tells him he's too young to train and shuts the door.

scenes.heading: EXT. PORCH - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Jack bursts out and yells at Ricky to leave, but Ricky remains motionless like a statue.

scenes.heading: EXT. PORCH - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack attacks Ricky with a broom and kicks his bag off the porch, but Ricky doesn't move.

scenes.heading: EXT. PORCH - MORNING
scenes.summary: Tyler comes out and politely tells the still-waiting Ricky to leave or he'll call the police.

scenes.heading: EXT. PORCH - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack attacks Ricky with the broom again, hitting him and screaming that he'll never get inside.

scenes.heading: INT. JACK'S ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Jack watches Ricky from his window while reflecting that they all became what Tyler wanted them to be.

scenes.heading: EXT. PORCH - MORNING
scenes.summary: Bob arrives and stands next to Ricky, while Tyler tells Ricky to get his stuff and tells Bob he's too old.

scenes.heading: INT. 2ND FLOOR LANDING - LATER
scenes.summary: Jack watches Tyler shave off all of Ricky's hair and call him a space monkey ready for Project Mayhem.

scenes.heading: EXT. PORCH - LATER
scenes.summary: Ricky comes out with the broom and attacks Bob, telling him he's too old and to get out.

scenes.heading: EXT. PORCH - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack watches from the window as a new space monkey attacks a skinny applicant with the broom.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - SAME
scenes.summary: Jack sees the living room filled with shaved-head space monkeys in black clothing wrapping soap at tables.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Space monkeys render fat and make soap in the kitchen while one recites Tyler's philosophy about not being unique snowflakes.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jack walks through the living room and heads upstairs.

scenes.heading: INT. TYLER'S ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jack discovers baskets labeled with cities containing financial documents, but Bob stops him and recites the first rule of Project Mayhem.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Jack drinks vodka among the working space monkeys when the phone rings and Tyler's voice tells him not to answer it.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT PORCH - LATER
scenes.summary: Jack, Bob and two space monkeys leave the house with boxes and equipment, heading for a van.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The group attaches explosive packets to a large metal globe atop a tower-like art installation in a park.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARK - LATER
scenes.summary: Bob detonates the explosives, bringing down the tower exactly as planned, with the globe rolling into the street and destroying a limo.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARK - AERIAL VIEW
scenes.summary: Jack and the others flee laughing from their successful demolition, splitting up and running in different directions as sirens approach.

scenes.heading: INT. CONCRETE STAIRCASE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jack discovers Bob's dead body with a bullet hole through his head, holding the detonator, as a cop spots Jack and draws his weapon.
scenes.turningPointNote: Bob's death marks the first major casualty of Project Mayhem and Jack's direct confrontation with the deadly consequences of Tyler's escalating violence

scenes.heading: EXT. PARK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jack flees through the park as police search for him, hearing radio chatter about finding his discarded clothes.

scenes.heading: EXT. PAPER ST. HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack returns home trembling to find space monkeys leaving with luggage and flight coupons, all bearing the kiss-scar on their hands.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jack watches a space monkey silently endure having his fingerprints burned off with lye while others wait their turn.

scenes.heading: INT. TYLER'S ROOM
scenes.summary: Jack searches Tyler's room and finds an answering machine with messages from Marla and Detective Stern about arson questioning.

scenes.heading: INT. BAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack searches for Tyler in a bar where bruised patrons and the bartender claim they've never heard of him while winking and smiling.

scenes.heading: INT. ANOTHER BAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A bartender with a neck brace tells Jack he was there last Thursday asking about security, questioning who Jack thinks he is.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jack begins to realize he may have been acting as Tyler without remembering, suggesting the split personality revelation is approaching

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack calls Marla from a hotel room in darkness, asking if they've ever had sex.

scenes.heading: INT. MARLA'S ROOM - SAME
scenes.summary: Marla confirms they've had sex and accuses Jack of fucking her then snubbing her, loving and hating her alternately.

scenes.heading: INT. BAR BASEMENT - NIGHT (JACK'S MEMORY)
scenes.summary: Jack remembers Tyler speaking at fight club, but the memory shifts to show Jack himself delivering Tyler's words in the spotlight.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jack begins to realize he and Tyler are the same person through altered flashbacks

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL ROOM - RESUMING
scenes.summary: Tyler explains to a gasping Jack that when Jack controls the body, he sees and hears Tyler, but no one else does.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE CABIN - IN FLIGHT - NIGHT (ALTERED FLASHBACK)
scenes.summary: In an altered flashback, Jack sits alone on the airplane talking to empty air where he believes Tyler is sitting.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL ROOM - RESUMING
scenes.summary: Jack asks Tyler about being together around other people, and Tyler reveals that Jack never talked during those times.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARK - NIGHT (ALTERED FLASHBACK)
scenes.summary: In another altered flashback, Jack confronts the Police Commissioner while behaving as Tyler, threatening to send body parts to newspapers.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL ROOM - RESUMING
scenes.summary: Tyler sits on the bed, takes Jack's hand, kisses his scar, and reveals he goes places and does things when Jack sleeps.

scenes.heading: EXT. BUILDING (FLASHBACK)
scenes.summary: In flashback, Jack leads a group rappelling down a TRW building while behaving as Tyler, spray-painting and inspiring others to shout mantras from broken windows.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL ROOM - RESUMING
scenes.summary: Jack questions Tyler about renting the house and his nature, leading to Tyler claiming Jack is his hallucination and challenging who has done more with their shared body.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Jack wakes up alone, remembers the previous night's revelations, looks at himself in the mirror, and sees it's 1:35 PM.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY
scenes.summary: Jack bursts out of the hotel room and sprints toward the stairs, abandoning his luggage.

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRWELL
scenes.summary: Jack races down the stairwell taking three steps at a time.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY
scenes.summary: Jack storms through the hotel lobby where a desk clerk shows him a bill with numerous phone calls made between 2 AM and 5:30 AM, which Jack initials before leaving.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE CABIN - IN FLIGHT - DAY
scenes.summary: Jack sits on an airplane staring out the window with a hard expression on his face.

scenes.heading: EXT. AIRPORT DRIVE - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Jack sprints from the airport and leaps into a taxi.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARLA'S HOTEL - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Jack's taxi arrives at Marla's hotel and he runs up the front steps.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL STAIRS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jack sprints up the hotel stairs taking multiple steps at a time to reach the landing.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY
scenes.summary: Jack pounds on Marla's door and she opens it, telling him Tyler's followers threw her out and nearly broke her arm, while Jack takes her hands and asks for her faith.

scenes.heading: INT. MARLA'S ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jack leads Marla to the bed and reveals Tyler is his split personality, showing her his real driver's license, but she disappears mid-conversation when he warns her about danger.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY
scenes.summary: Jack leaps out of the room and looks down the hallway in both directions calling for Marla.

scenes.heading: EXT. PAPER STREET - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Jack's taxi screeches to a halt on Paper Street and he leaps out, telling the driver to wait.

scenes.heading: INT. PAPER ST. HOUSE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Jack enters the Paper Street house to find it completely empty and deserted, seeing only bathtubs, canisters of liquid, and bottles labeled nitric acid.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN
scenes.summary: Jack calls phone numbers from the hotel bill, reaching maintenance departments across the city who recognize him and confirm they're ready for their mission as the sun sets.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jack discovers Tyler has coordinated a city-wide operation through maintenance departments while he slept

scenes.heading: EXT. LOU'S TAVERN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack gets out of a cab at Lou's Tavern and the cabbie calls him Mr. Durden, causing Jack to back away in shock.

scenes.heading: INT. LOU'S TAVERN - SAME
scenes.summary: Jack asks Irvine about the nitroglycerin's location, but Irvine treats it as a test Tyler warned him about, leading Jack to grab his collar in rage.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOU'S TAVERN
scenes.summary: Jack ignores the waiting cabbie and walks briskly down the street with psychotic determination, pulling Marla with him into a side street.

scenes.heading: INT. PAWN SHOP
scenes.summary: Jack enters a pawn shop, distracts the owner by asking for a trumpet, then steals a loaded handgun from behind the counter and flees with Marla.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jack carjacks a Lexus at gunpoint, forcing the driver to flee, then threatens Marla into the car and speeds away.

scenes.heading: EXT. GREYHOUND BUS STATION - LATER
scenes.summary: Jack drags Marla to a Greyhound bus station and forces her onto a bus while deliberately avoiding seeing the destination sign.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack notices Space Monkeys watching him from multiple street corners and realizes they're tracking his movements like a migrating animal.

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE STATION - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack walks through a police station past plainclothes detectives until he finds a private office.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jack tells Detective Stern he wants to be arrested as the leader of a terrorist organization about to bomb the city.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Jack is led through the hallway in handcuffs by Detective Stern and several other detectives in a somber mood.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERROGATION ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The detectives lead Jack into an interrogation room where they burst into hysterical laughter, congratulating him on Operation Domino and treating his confession as part of the plan.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jack discovers the police are part of Tyler's operation, eliminating his last hope of stopping the plan

scenes.heading: EXT. BACK OF POLICE STATION - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack is playfully shoved out the back door of the police station as detectives laugh behind him.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jack walks into the street in shock, realizes Operation Domino means buildings will fall like dominoes, and deduces the first target is Citibank.

scenes.heading: INT. STOLEN LEXUS - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Jack races through the streets in the stolen Lexus, swerving and honking, while a motorcycle cop provides him an escort instead of stopping him.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITIBANK BUILDING - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jack leaps out of the car and sprints into the Citibank building.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Two Space Monkeys escort Jack toward the elevators in the lobby, but he darts to the stairwell instead.

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRWELL
scenes.summary: Jack dashes down the stairwell steps.

scenes.heading: INT. PARKING AREA
scenes.summary: Jack frantically searches the parking garage until he finds Tyler sitting against a support post, who reveals there are ten minutes left and eight floors of charges to find.

scenes.heading: INT. ELEVATOR
scenes.summary: Jack and Tyler fight for the gun inside an elevator as it rapidly climbs floors, with Tyler retaining control of the weapon.

scenes.heading: INT. TOP FLOOR LOBBY
scenes.summary: Tyler shoves Jack's head against the window to show him a Greyhound bus idling by the front doors below.

scenes.heading: INT. TOP FLOOR LOBBY - RESUMING
scenes.summary: Jack denies the bus connection while Tyler confirms it, then Jack aims the gun at Tyler and fires, missing, declaring he'll kill Tyler instead of himself.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jack decides to kill Tyler rather than himself, beginning the final confrontation between his split personalities

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY
scenes.summary: Jack chases Tyler down a hallway and aims the gun at him, while security camera footage shows Jack aiming the gun at himself.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY
scenes.summary: Jack fires at Tyler who flinches, while the security camera shows Jack shooting at himself and flinching.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY
scenes.summary: Jack continues sprinting until he turns a corner into a new section.

scenes.heading: INT. NEW SECTION OF HALLWAY
scenes.summary: Jack fires at Tyler and grazes his leg, while security camera footage shows Jack falling backwards with a graze wound he gave himself.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY
scenes.summary: Jack pulls himself to his feet with an insane expression and runs with a limp, holding the gun ready to fire.

scenes.heading: INT. TOP FLOOR LOBBY
scenes.summary: Tyler leaps onto Jack in the lobby, while security camera footage shows Jack wrestling with himself, punching and kicking his own body.

scenes.heading: INT. TOP FLOOR LOBBY
scenes.summary: Tyler leaps to his feet and runs away while Jack pulls himself up and follows.

scenes.heading: INT. LARGE SOCIAL ROOM
scenes.summary: Tyler seizes Jack's gun and shoves the barrel into Jack's mouth, threatening to kill them both as martyrs while Jack collapses exhausted to the floor in a room filled with duffel bags overlooking the city.
scenes.turningPointNote: The final physical confrontation between Tyler and Jack reaches its climax with Tyler holding a gun in Jack's mouth, forcing the ultimate crisis of identity and survival.
